---
title: "Numerical Analysis"
subtitle: "Foundations and Exercises"
author:
  - name: "TANAKA, Kazuaki (田中 一成)"
    email: "k.tanaka@aoni.waseda.jp"
    affiliations:
      - name: "Global Center for Science and Engineering, Waseda University"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, ../030/custom.scss]
    slide-number: true
    html-math-method: mathjax
    chalkboard:
      storage: chalkboardData_12th
    title-slide-attributes:
      data-background-image: "../030/images/image1.png"
      data-background-size: "contain"
      data-background-opacity: "0.15"
---

## Before We Start {.smaller}

```{=html}
<div class="mission-tip" style="margin-top:0.6em; margin-bottom:0.8em;">
  <div class="mission-tip-icon">📥</div>
  <div class="mission-tip-content">
    <strong>Please pull the latest course materials repository.</strong>
  </div>
</div>

<div style="display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:0.5em; margin-bottom:0.8em;">
  <div>
    <div style="font-size:0.78em; color:#888; margin-bottom:0.2em;">Already cloned</div>
    <pre style="margin:0; background:#1e1e2e; color:#cdd6f4; border-radius:8px; padding:0.5em 1em; font-size:0.78em; line-height:1.5;"><code>cd materials
git pull</code></pre>
  </div>
</div>

<div style="background:#f0f4ff; border-left:3px solid #4472C4; border-radius:6px; padding:0.6em 1em; font-size:0.82em;">
  <strong>Today's materials</strong>:
  <table style="margin-top:0.4em; border-collapse:collapse; width:100%;">
    <tr><td style="padding:0.15em 0.6em 0.15em 0;"><code>120/12th.qmd</code></td><td style="color:#555;">slides — logistics and assignments</td></tr>
    <tr><td style="padding:0.15em 0.6em 0.15em 0;"><code>100/integration-handout.qmd</code></td><td style="color:#555;">📘 <strong>shared handout for Lectures 10--12</strong></td></tr>
    <tr><td style="padding:0.15em 0.6em 0.15em 0;"><code>100/integration-handout.html</code></td><td style="color:#555;">rendered shared handout</td></tr>
    <tr><td style="padding:0.15em 0.6em 0.15em 0;"><code>120/assignment/README.md</code></td><td style="color:#555;">repo README for Ex 12 (copy to Classroom repo root)</td></tr>
    <tr><td style="padding:0.15em 0.6em 0.15em 0;"><code>120/assignment/ex12-1.qmd</code></td><td style="color:#555;">exercise template for Ex 12.1</td></tr>
    <tr><td style="padding:0.15em 0.6em 0.15em 0;"><code>120/assignment/ex12-2.qmd</code></td><td style="color:#555;">exercise template for Ex 12.2</td></tr>
  </table>
</div>
```

## Today's Main Material {.smaller}

```{=html}
<div style="background:#e8f5e9; border-left:4px solid #2e7d32; border-radius:8px; padding:0.8em 1em; margin-bottom:0.7em;">
  <div style="font-size:0.82em; color:#1b5e20; font-weight:700; letter-spacing:0.04em;">Lecture 12</div>
  <div style="font-size:1.05em; margin-top:0.2em;"><strong>Numerical Integration III: Hermite Interpolation and Gaussian Quadrature</strong></div>
  <div style="font-size:0.84em; color:#455a64; margin-top:0.25em;">
    Use the shared Lectures 10--12 handout. Today we focus on Sections 6--7.
  </div>
</div>

<div style="display:grid; grid-template-columns:1fr; gap:0.8em;">
  <div style="background:#fff8e1; border:1px solid #ffe082; border-radius:10px; padding:0.75em 0.9em;">
    <div style="font-size:0.8em; color:#e65100; font-weight:700;">Today's scope</div>
    <ul style="font-size:0.83em; line-height:1.55; margin:0.4em 0 0 1.2em;">
      <li>Hermite interpolation</li>
      <li>inner products and orthogonal polynomials</li>
      <li>Gauss-Chebyshev quadrature</li>
      <li>Gauss-Legendre quadrature</li>
    </ul>
  </div>
</div>
```

## Exercise 12.0 — Evolve the Shared Handout with AI {.smaller .exercise-slide}

<div style="padding-left: 2em; margin-top: 0.5em; margin-bottom: 0.4em;">
  <strong>Repository:</strong> <a href="https://classroom.github.com/a/lbBXM8pn">Ex 10 — GitHub Classroom</a>
  &nbsp;&nbsp;|&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong>Submit:</strong> <code>integration-handout.qmd</code> (your edited copy)
  &nbsp;&nbsp;|&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong style="color: #d84315;">Deadline: 7/9</strong>
</div>

Continue from your Ex 10.0 / Ex 11.0 work on
<code>integration-handout.qmd</code> in the same repository.
Add at least **3 additional Q&A blocks** on **§6--7** (Hermite interpolation,
Gaussian quadrature) using the protocol in
[`AI_TUTOR.md`](https://github.com/waseda-num-analysis-2026/materials/blob/main/AI_TUTOR.md),
and **keep your prior Q&A** from earlier exercises.

## Exercise 12.1 — Gauss-Chebyshev Quadrature {.smaller .exercise-slide}

<div style="padding-left: 2em; margin-top: 0.5em; margin-bottom: 0.4em;">
  <strong>Format:</strong> Individual Work
  &nbsp;&nbsp;|&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong>Repository:</strong> same as Ex 10.0
  &nbsp;&nbsp;|&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong>Edit:</strong> <code>ex12-1.qmd</code>
  &nbsp;&nbsp;|&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong style="color: #d84315;">Deadline: 7/9</strong>
</div>

After `git pull`, copy all 3 files from `120/assignment/` to your repo root (overwrite `README.md`):

`cp 120/assignment/{README.md,ex12-1.qmd,ex12-2.qmd} /path/to/your-ex10-12-repo/`

See repo `README.md` for details.

Implement **Gauss-Chebyshev quadrature** for numerical integration.

**Tasks:**

- Complete the `gauss_chebyshev` function in `ex12-1.qmd`
- Choose a smooth integrand and state the problem clearly
- Execute the quadrature for $n = 1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32$ and report approximations and errors
- Plot error vs. number of nodes $n$ and discuss convergence behaviour

## Exercise 12.2 — Numerical Integration of Your Chosen Function {.smaller .exercise-slide}

<div style="padding-left: 2em; margin-top: 0.5em; margin-bottom: 0.3em;">
  <strong>Format:</strong> Individual Work
  &nbsp;&nbsp;|&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong>Repository:</strong> same as Ex 10.0
  &nbsp;&nbsp;|&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong>Edit:</strong> <code>ex12-2.qmd</code>
  &nbsp;&nbsp;|&nbsp;&nbsp; <strong style="color: #d84315;">Deadline: 7/9</strong><br>
  Same copy step as Ex 12.1 — see repo <code>README.md</code> for details
</div>

Choose any smooth $f(x)$; compare **trapezoidal**, **Simpson**, and **Gauss-Legendre** quadrature.

**Tasks:**

- Choose $f(x)$, interval $[a,b]$, and exact (or reference) integral
- Apply trapezoidal and Simpson rules from earlier exercises
- Apply Gauss-Legendre (AI OK for nodes/weights)
- Table + convergence plot; brief discussion of efficiency

## Assignments and Preparation {.smaller}

```{=html}
<div class="assign-list">
  <div class="assign-card">
    <div class="assign-num">1</div>
    <div class="assign-body">
      <p>Use the same <a href="https://classroom.github.com/a/lbBXM8pn"><strong>Ex 10 GitHub Classroom</strong></a> repository from Lectures 10--11.</p>
      <ul>
        <li>After <code>git pull</code>, copy <code>120/assignment/README.md</code>, <code>ex12-1.qmd</code>, and <code>ex12-2.qmd</code> to your repo root — <strong>overwrite</strong> <code>README.md</code> (<code>cp 120/assignment/{README.md,ex12-1.qmd,ex12-2.qmd} /path/to/your-ex10-12-repo/</code>)</li>
        <li>For <strong>Ex 12.0</strong>: continue editing <code>integration-handout.qmd</code>, add &ge; 3 Q&amp;A blocks on &sect;6--7, and keep your prior Q&amp;A from Ex 10.0 / Ex 11.0</li>
        <li>For <strong>Ex 12.1</strong> and <strong>Ex 12.2</strong>: edit the copied <code>ex12-1.qmd</code> and <code>ex12-2.qmd</code> (details in repo <code>README.md</code>)</li>
        <li><strong>IMPORTANT:</strong> render each submission file and push the rendered output together with any <code>*_files/</code> directories</li>
        <li><strong>IMPORTANT:</strong> after pushing, open your GitHub repository in a browser and confirm the rendered files are visible</li>
        <li>Report AI use as instructed in the assignment template</li>
        <li><strong>Deadline:</strong> Ex 12.0, Ex 12.1, and Ex 12.2 — <span class="assign-due">7/9</span></li>
      </ul>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
```
